title: cassine reticulata
intro: Cassine reticulata, also known as the netleaf cassine or netleaf sumac, is a shrub native to the southeastern United States, belonging to the Anacardiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cassine reticulata include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and a digestive aid. Cassine reticulata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various herbal preparations.
